title: Bulk-Boundary Correspondence To Fermi Arc
summary: The same bulk topology that assigns charges to Weyl nodes reorganizes boundary spectra into open Fermi arcs, so the arc is a boundary projection of changing bulk topological data.

title: Continuum Anomaly To Lattice No-Go
summary: The continuum fact that a lone charged chiral fermion is anomalous motivates the lattice theorem that a globally consistent band structure cannot realize an unmatched net chirality.

title: Weyl Node To Berry Monopole
summary: A Weyl node can be read either as a linearized band crossing or as a momentum-space monopole of Berry curvature whose flux equals the node charge.

title: Integrating Out the Emergent Field Is Only Locally Valid
summary: Solving f = F / r for a reproduces the Hall current locally but fails globally because flux quantization obstructs eliminating a for arbitrary A; source anchor: Lecture Two / Quantization Of The Hall Conductivity (lines 1409-1427; eqs. yre, zyre).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two caveat-card audit for the local-only validity of integrating out the emergent field.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two caveat-card audit for the local-only validity of integrating out the emergent field.

title: Fermi Arcs Terminate At Projected Weyl Nodes
summary: The endpoints of a Fermi arc in the surface Brillouin zone are fixed by the projections of bulk Weyl nodes that source the changing topological data across momentum slices.

title: Generic 2D Dirac Points Require Symmetry Protection
summary: Unlike isolated two-band crossings in three dimensions, two-dimensional Dirac points are generally not stable without extra symmetry or special structure.

title: Isolated 3D Two-Band Crossings Are Weyl Nodes
summary: A generic isolated two-band crossing in three dimensions linearizes to a Weyl Hamiltonian, with chirality given by the sign of the Jacobian determinant.

title: Lattice Weyl Nodes Sum To Zero Net Chirality
summary: In a lattice system with isolated Weyl nodes, the total chirality over the Brillouin zone vanishes; positive and negative nodes must balance globally.

title: Band Crossing
summary: A band crossing is a momentum-space point where two energy bands become degenerate; in Lecture One it is the local seed from which Weyl and Dirac low-energy descriptions are extracted.

title: Berry Connection
summary: The Berry connection is the U(1) connection on the line bundle of occupied states over regular momentum space, encoding phase transport of eigenstates along paths.

title: Berry Curvature
summary: Berry curvature is the curvature two-form of the Berry connection; in Lecture One its flux through a sphere around a bad point measures the node's topological charge.

title: Chirality Of A Weyl Node
summary: The chirality of a Weyl node is the sign of the Jacobian relating momentum deviations to the Pauli-vector coefficients in the local Hamiltonian, equivalently the sign of its Berry-flux charge.

title: Complex Line Bundle Of Filled States
summary: For a two-band system with one filled band, the occupied state at each regular momentum defines a one-dimensional complex vector space; together these fibers form the line bundle on which the Berry connection lives.

title: Dirac Point In Two Dimensions
summary: A two-dimensional Dirac point is a linear band crossing in 2D whose generic persistence usually requires extra symmetry or special tuning rather than only codimension counting.

title: Fermi Arc
summary: A Fermi arc is a surface-state locus in boundary momentum space whose endpoints lie at the projections of bulk Weyl nodes of opposite total charge.

title: Nielsen-Ninomiya Theorem
summary: For a lattice band structure with isolated Weyl crossings, the sum of all node charges over the Brillouin zone is zero, forbidding a net chirality in a strictly lattice-regularized system.

title: Parity Anomaly of a Single Two Plus One Dimensional Dirac Fermion
summary: A single 2+1-dimensional Dirac fermion cannot be regulated so as to preserve both large-gauge invariance and parity; choosing gauge invariance leaves a parity-odd Chern-Simons term whose coefficient is half-integer modulo integers.

title: Wave-Function Topology as a Classification Axis Beyond Symmetry Breaking
summary: Topological quantum matter is framed as phases distinguished by topologically nontrivial quantum-mechanical wave functions rather than by spontaneous symmetry breaking or local order parameters; source anchor: Introduction / Opening motivation and topology-symmetry contrast and Introduction / Scope and organization of the review (lines 187-230, 331-335).

coverage.notes: Bounded Introduction audit for the wave-function-topology versus symmetry-breaking distinction from Chiu et al. 1505.03535.

consensus.notes: Bounded Introduction audit for the wave-function-topology versus symmetry-breaking distinction from Chiu et al. 1505.03535.

title: Weyl Node
summary: A Weyl node is an isolated three-dimensional two-band crossing whose linearized Hamiltonian is a Weyl Hamiltonian and whose local topological charge is nonzero.

title: Abelian Chern-Simons Functional in Two Plus One Dimensions
summary: The gauge-invariant-up-to-boundary term I_CS = (1/4pi) integral epsilon^{ijk} A_i partial_j A_k is the distinguished 2+1-dimensional response term; source anchor: Lecture Two / Chern-Simons Effective Action (lines 1273-1285; eq. worf).

coverage.notes: Bounded definition-card audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded definition-card audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Fractional Hall Effective Action with an Emergent U(1) Field
summary: The macroscopic fractional Hall response is described by a mixed A da term together with a level-r Chern-Simons term for a; source anchor: Lecture Two / Quantization Of The Hall Conductivity (lines 1398-1407; eq. urr).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two definition-card audit for the fractional Hall effective action with an emergent U(1) field.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two definition-card audit for the fractional Hall effective action with an emergent U(1) field.

title: Nearest-Neighbor Graphene Hamiltonian
summary: The honeycomb nearest-neighbor hopping model gives H = t [[0, 1 + e^{-i alpha} + e^{-i beta}], [1 + e^{i alpha} + e^{i beta}, 0]] in the A/B basis; source anchor: Lecture Three / Haldane's Model Of Graphene (lines 2192-2203; eq. hham).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Three graphene Hamiltonian audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Three graphene Hamiltonian audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Many-Band Filled Subspace as a Grassmannian Map
summary: For an N-band system with k filled bands, the occupied subspace H'_p defines a map into Gr(k,N) away from crossings of the k-th and k+1-th bands; source anchor: Lecture One / A System With Many Bands (lines 742-766).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One many-band occupied-subspace audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One many-band occupied-subspace audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Many-Body Twist-Angle Chern Number
summary: Twisted boundary phases alpha_1 and alpha_2 define a torus of adiabatic parameters with Berry curvature hat F whose integral gives the many-body integer hat k'; source anchor: Lecture Two / Relation To Band Topology (lines 1468-1500).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two many-body twist-angle Chern-number audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two many-body twist-angle Chern-number audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Chirality Operator in One Plus One Dimensions
summary: In 1+1 dimensions, gamma_0 gamma_1 is the chirality operator and its eigenvalue distinguishes right-moving from left-moving gapless modes; source anchor: Lecture One / Relativistic Dispersion In One Space Dimension (lines 328-350; eqs. Dirac, gamma, hamdir).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One chirality-operator audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One chirality-operator audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Spatial Versus Nonspatial Symmetry Split
summary: The paper distinguishes nonspatial symmetries as symmetries acting locally in position space and formalizes the split by calling a symmetry spatial when it acts on spatial indices and nonspatial when it does not; on-site symmetries are a special nonspatial case; source anchor: Introduction / Overview of topological materials and Symmetries / Spatial-vs-nonspatial split (lines 259-263, 534-545).

coverage.notes: Bounded symmetry-definition audit for the spatial-versus-nonspatial split.

consensus.notes: Bounded symmetry-definition audit for the spatial-versus-nonspatial split.

title: Introduction-Level Definition of a Symmetry-Protected Topological Phase
summary: The review introduces topological insulators as symmetry-protected topological phases: short-range-entangled gapped phases whose topological properties rely on symmetry, since without time-reversal symmetry they can be adiabatically deformed to trivial insulators without closing the bulk gap; source anchor: Introduction / Opening motivation and topology-symmetry contrast (lines 220-229).

coverage.notes: Bounded Introduction definition audit for the rough SPT-phase characterization.

consensus.notes: Bounded Introduction definition audit for the rough SPT-phase characterization.

title: TKNN Invariant as the First Chern Class of the Filled-Band Bundle
summary: For a gapped two-dimensional band insulator, the integer k' is the first Chern class of the filled-band bundle, equivalently the integral of Tr F / 2pi over the Brillouin zone; source anchor: Lecture Two / Relation To Band Topology (lines 1429-1451).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two TKNN-as-Chern-class audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two TKNN-as-Chern-class audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Topological Equivalence of Insulating States
summary: Two insulating states are topologically equivalent when the Hamiltonian can be tuned continuously between them without closing the energy gap; source anchor: Topological Band Theory / The insulating state (lines 174-177).

coverage.notes: Bounded definition audit for the gap-preserving interpolation criterion that defines topological equivalence between insulating states.

consensus.notes: Bounded definition audit for the gap-preserving interpolation criterion that defines topological equivalence between insulating states.

title: Topological Insulator as a Bulk Gap with Protected Boundary States
summary: A topological insulator has a bulk energy gap separating occupied and empty bands, but its edge or surface necessarily carries gapless states protected by time-reversal symmetry; source anchor: Introduction (lines 83-90).

coverage.notes: Bounded introduction audit for the early Hasan-Kane definition of a topological insulator as a bulk-gapped state with symmetry-protected boundary modes.

consensus.notes: Bounded introduction audit for the early Hasan-Kane definition of a topological insulator as a bulk-gapped state with symmetry-protected boundary modes.

title: Hermiticity-Preserving Weyl Boundary Condition
summary: A half-space Weyl fermion admits boundary data M psi = psi with M = sigma_2 cos alpha + sigma_3 sin alpha, chosen so that H remains Hermitian; source anchor: Lecture One / Weyl Fermions And Fermi Arcs (lines 883-899; eqs. goodbc, mis, zeff).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One Weyl half-space boundary-condition audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One Weyl half-space boundary-condition audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Wilson Loop Operator in Level-r U(1) Chern-Simons Theory
summary: Because the classical equation sets f = 0, the relevant gauge-invariant observables are Wilson loops W_s(l) = exp(i s integral_l a) rather than local fields; source anchor: Lecture Three / More On The Fractional Quantum Hall Effect (lines 1976-1989; eqs. doof, ooff).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Three Wilson-loop definition audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Three Wilson-loop definition audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Berry Flux Gives Node Charge
summary: Once the occupied states are organized into a line bundle, the flux of Berry curvature through a small sphere around a bad point yields the node's integer topological charge.

title: Linearization Gives A 1D Chiral Mode
summary: Linearizing the dispersion around a one-dimensional Fermi crossing and replacing momentum deviation by -i d/dx yields a continuum chiral Hamiltonian whose sign of velocity fixes the branch direction.

title: Stokes's Theorem Implies Net Zero Charge
summary: Because the relevant form is closed on the punctured Brillouin zone, integrating its exterior derivative over the punctured region and applying Stokes's theorem yields that the sum of boundary charges around all bad points is zero.

title: The First-Order Adiabatic Action Equals the Berry Connection
summary: In the adiabatic limit only the one-derivative term survives, so the classical connection A_i(x) dx^i becomes the Berry connection for the ground-state transport problem; source anchor: Lecture Two / Proof Of The Equivalence (lines 1540-1569; eqs. merrof, monkey, wonkey).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two adiabatic Berry-connection derivation audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two adiabatic Berry-connection derivation audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Boundary Dirac Mode from Negative Bulk Mass
summary: For a Dirac fermion in a half-space, psi = exp(m x_1) psi_parallel is normalizable only for m < 0 and yields a localized 2+1-dimensional boundary Dirac mode; source anchor: Lecture One / Gapless Boundary Modes From Dirac Fermions (lines 1020-1056; eqs. molof, ogg, pogg, zuffo).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One boundary Dirac-mode derivation audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One boundary Dirac-mode derivation audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Charge Pumping Counts Edge Chirality
summary: Under alpha -> alpha + 2pi, spectral flow along edge branches transfers one unit of charge per right-mover and minus one per left-mover, so k = n_+ - n_-; source anchor: Lecture Two / The Charge Pump and Joining Valence And Conduction Bands (lines 1635-1753; eqs. zimmo, trof).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two charge-pump audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two charge-pump audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: A Domain-Wall Mass Yields a Chiral Edge Mode
summary: After flipping one fermion mass so that the doubled system has m sign(x_1), the Jackiw-Rebbi solution localizes a chiral mode at the interface; source anchor: Lecture Three / More On Edge States Of The Integer Quantum Hall Effect (lines 2106-2151; eqs. plift, nuft, gift, zoggg, trogg).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Three domain-wall edge-mode audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Three domain-wall edge-mode audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Fermi Arcs from an Auxiliary Two-Torus Chern Number
summary: The product U x U' around a projected Weyl point defines a gapped auxiliary 2D system whose Chern number k* forces edge states and hence Fermi arcs in the original 3D problem; source anchor: Lecture Two / More On Fermi Arcs (lines 1788-1846).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two auxiliary-torus Fermi-arc argument audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two auxiliary-torus Fermi-arc argument audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Fractional Quasiparticle Charge q over r
summary: From the a_0 equation of motion and J_0 = f_12 / 2pi, a quasiparticle of integer a-charge q carries ordinary electric charge q / r; source anchor: Lecture Three / More On The Fractional Quantum Hall Effect (lines 1863-1906; eqs. pelf, qof, pefr).

coverage.notes: Bounded derivation-step audit for the fractional charge argument.

consensus.notes: Bounded derivation-step audit for the fractional charge argument.

title: Graphene Dirac Points Solve 1 + e^{i alpha} + e^{i beta} = 0
summary: Band crossings occur exactly when 1 + e^{i alpha} + e^{i beta} = 0, yielding the two honeycomb Dirac points; source anchor: Lecture Three / Haldane's Model Of Graphene (lines 2207-2213; eqs. theq, omeq).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Three graphene Dirac-point audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Three graphene Dirac-point audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Hall Current from Functional Variation of k I_CS
summary: Varying the effective action gives J_2 = - delta I_eff / delta A_1 = k E_1 / 2pi, the quantized Hall current; source anchor: Lecture Two / Quantization Of The Hall Conductivity (lines 1367-1386; eqs. curr, zurr, murr).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two derivation-step audit for the Hall current obtained from varying k I_CS.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two derivation-step audit for the Hall current obtained from varying k I_CS.

title: Local Weyl Hamiltonian Near an Isolated Crossing
summary: Expanding b_i(p) near p* gives H' = sum_ij sigma_i b_ij (p-p*)_j, the local Weyl Hamiltonian governing the crossing; source anchor: Lecture One / Three Dimensions (lines 394-450; eqs. localexp, expa, xpa, irha).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One local-Weyl audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One local-Weyl audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Surface Bound-State Ray as the Seed of a Fermi Arc
summary: Solving the half-space Weyl equation gives exponentially localized surface modes along a momentum-space ray, providing the local seed of a Fermi arc; source anchor: Lecture One / Weyl Fermions And Fermi Arcs (lines 911-947; eqs. polk, formix, molt).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One local Fermi-arc seed derivation audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One local Fermi-arc seed derivation audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Berry Flux Equals Topological Charge
summary: For an isolated bad point, the integral of Berry curvature over a small surrounding sphere equals the integer topological charge of the node.

title: Continuum 1D Chiral Fermion Hamiltonian
summary: After replacing p-p0 by -i d/dx, the linearized one-dimensional crossing becomes a continuum Hamiltonian H=v integral dx psi* (-i d/dx) psi up to an irrelevant constant shift.

title: First-Quantized Chiral Symmetry Constraint
summary: Chiral symmetry, obtained as the product of time-reversal and particle-hole symmetry, constrains a first-quantized Hamiltonian by U_S^dagger H U_S = -H and, after phase choice, {H,U_S} = 0 with U_S^2 = 1, yielding a block-off-diagonal form in the chiral basis; source anchor: Symmetries / Chiral symmetry (lines 775-850; Eq. chiral_Def_eq).

coverage.notes: Bounded chiral equation-card audit for the first-quantized symmetry constraint.

consensus.notes: Bounded chiral equation-card audit for the first-quantized symmetry constraint.

title: First-Quantized Particle-Hole Symmetry Constraint
summary: For a noninteracting first-quantized Hamiltonian, particle-hole symmetry imposes U_C^dagger H^T U_C = -H, equivalently -U_C^dagger H^* U_C = H, and the generic symmetry-class analysis yields U_C^* U_C = +/- 1; source anchor: Symmetries / Particle-hole symmetry (lines 675-724; Eq. DEFChargeConjugation).

coverage.notes: Bounded PHS equation-card audit for the first-quantized symmetry constraint.

consensus.notes: Bounded PHS equation-card audit for the first-quantized symmetry constraint.

title: First-Quantized Time-Reversal Symmetry Constraint
summary: For a noninteracting first-quantized Hamiltonian, time-reversal symmetry imposes U_T^dagger H^* U_T = H, and the generic symmetry-class analysis yields U_T^* U_T = +/- 1, determining whether T^2 acts as identity or fermion parity; source anchor: Symmetries / Time-reversal symmetry (lines 553-669; Eq. DEFTimeReversal).

coverage.notes: Bounded TRS equation-card audit for the first-quantized symmetry constraint.

consensus.notes: Bounded TRS equation-card audit for the first-quantized symmetry constraint.

title: Integer Quantum Hall Conductivity
summary: If N Landau levels are filled and the rest are empty, the Hall conductivity is sigma_xy = N e^2 / h; source anchor: Topological Band Theory / The quantum Hall state (lines 217-224, Eq. qhall).

coverage.notes: Bounded equation audit for the introductory integer quantum Hall conductivity formula used as Hasan-Kane's first topological counterexample.

consensus.notes: Bounded equation audit for the introductory integer quantum Hall conductivity formula used as Hasan-Kane's first topological counterexample.

title: Linearized Dispersion Near A Fermi Point
summary: Near an isolated one-dimensional Fermi crossing, the band energy expands linearly as epsilon(p)=epsilon(p0)+v(p-p0)+O((p-p0)^2).

title: Linearized One-Dimensional Fermi Crossing
summary: Near p0, epsilon(p) = epsilon(p0) + v(p-p0) + O((p-p0)^2) produces the local chiral continuum dispersion; source anchor: Lecture One / Relativistic Dispersion In One Space Dimension (lines 285-317; eqs. dolz, continuum).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One equation-card audit for the local linearized one-dimensional Fermi crossing.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One equation-card audit for the local linearized one-dimensional Fermi crossing.

title: Two-Band Hamiltonian A Plus B Dot Sigma
summary: A generic local two-band Hamiltonian can be written as H(p)=a(p)+b(p).sigma, and after subtracting the scalar part the crossing structure is governed by the vector b(p).
aliases: a plus b dot sigma form

title: Two-Band Weyl Model With A Tunable Pair
summary: The toy Hamiltonian with off-diagonal p1 plus or minus i p2 and diagonal plus or minus f(p3) realizes Weyl nodes whose creation, motion, and annihilation are controlled by the zeros of f.

title: Wilson-Loop Commutator from Intersection Number
summary: For loops with nonzero intersection number, the Wilson operators obey W_s(l) W_s'(l') = exp(2pi i s s' l intersect l' / r) W_s'(l') W_s(l), reducing to AB = exp(2pi i / r) BA in the basic torus case; source anchor: Lecture Three / More On The Fractional Quantum Hall Effect (lines 2022-2036; eqs. grooff, woof).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Three Wilson-loop commutator audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Three Wilson-loop commutator audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Winding Number As The Degree Of S2 To S2
summary: The topological charge of an isolated bad point is the degree of the map from a small enclosing sphere in momentum space to the target sphere of normalized b-vectors.

title: Winding-Number Integral for a Two-Band Node
summary: The node winding number is given by the sphere integral of n dot (partial n x partial n); source anchor: Lecture One / The Nielsen-Ninomiya Theorem (lines 516-525; eqs. wn, nwn).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One equation-card audit for the winding-number integral around an isolated two-band node.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One equation-card audit for the winding-number integral around an isolated two-band node.

title: First Chern Class from Berry Curvature
summary: The Berry curvature F represents the first Chern class through c1(L) <-> F / 2pi; source anchor: Lecture One / The Berry Connection (lines 588-592; eq. fc).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One equivalence-map audit for the c_1(L) <-> F / 2 pi identification.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One equivalence-map audit for the c_1(L) <-> F / 2 pi identification.

title: Forgetful Functor from Real to Complex AZ Classes
summary: Forgetting the antiunitary symmetries maps the real classes AI, D, AII, and C to complex class A and the real classes BDI, DIII, CI, and CII to complex class AIII; the resulting complex normalization explains why the s - delta = 4 real entry is labeled 2Z relative to the corresponding complex Z classification; source anchor: Fully gapped free fermion systems and topological defects / Topological defects (lines 2024-2030).

coverage.notes: Bounded equivalence audit for the forgetful-functor map from real to complex AZ classes.

consensus.notes: Bounded equivalence audit for the forgetful-functor map from real to complex AZ classes.

title: The Many-Body Integer hat k' Equals the Band Chern Number k'
summary: Integrating over the little twist-angle torus while summing shifted momenta reorganizes the same calculation as integrating over the full Brillouin zone, so hat k' = k'; source anchor: Lecture Two / Proof Of The Equivalence (lines 1523-1537).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two many-body to band Chern-number equivalence audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two many-body to band Chern-number equivalence audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Weyl Charge Equals Berry Flux
summary: The local node charge equals the Berry-flux integral over a small sphere, and the Bianchi identity then re-derives zero net charge; source anchor: Lecture One / The Berry Connection (lines 603-609; eqs. zif, nna).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One Berry-flux equivalence audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One Berry-flux equivalence audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Berry-Flux Diagnosis Of Node Charge
summary: To determine the charge of an isolated Weyl node, surround it by a small sphere in momentum space, compute the Berry curvature of the occupied state, and integrate the flux over the sphere.

title: Two-Band Lattice Weyl Model
summary: A simple periodic two-band lattice Hamiltonian provides an explicit realization of Weyl nodes on a Brillouin torus and makes the global doubling constraint visible.

title: Bloch-BdG Eigenfunction Notation for Topological Invariants
summary: The Topological invariants subsection introduces the invariant discussion in terms of Bloch-BdG eigenfunctions |u^a(k,r)> and energies epsilon^a(k,r) defined by H(k,r)|u^a(k,r)> = epsilon^a(k,r)|u^a(k,r)>; source anchor: Topological invariants / Bloch-BdG eigenfunction setup (lines 2041-2068).

coverage.notes: Bounded notation audit for the eigenfunction setup that underlies the invariant formulas.

consensus.notes: Bounded notation audit for the eigenfunction setup that underlies the invariant formulas.

title: Bloch States as Eigenstates of the Bloch Hamiltonian
summary: Band theory describes crystal states by crystal momentum k in the Brillouin zone, with Bloch states |u_m(k)> defined in a unit cell as eigenstates of the Bloch Hamiltonian H(k), and eigenvalues E_m(k) defining the energy bands; source anchor: Topological Band Theory / The insulating state (lines 161-168).

coverage.notes: Bounded notation audit for the Bloch-state setup that Hasan-Kane uses before introducing topological invariants.

consensus.notes: Bounded notation audit for the Bloch-state setup that Hasan-Kane uses before introducing topological invariants.

title: Emergent U(1) Field a and Its Field Strength f
summary: The fractional Hall discussion introduces an emergent compact U(1) gauge field a with field strength f_ij = partial_i a_j - partial_j a_i and the same 2pi flux quantum normalization as A; source anchor: Lecture Two / Quantization Of The Hall Conductivity (lines 1392-1407; eq. urr and adjoining discussion).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two notation-card audit for the emergent gauge field a and its field strength f.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two notation-card audit for the emergent gauge field a and its field strength f.

title: Filled Line Bundle and Berry Connection
summary: Away from bad points the filled state space L_p forms a complex line bundle equipped with Berry parallel transport defined by <psi_p, d psi_p / ds> = 0; source anchor: Lecture One / The Berry Connection (lines 558-582; eq. zb).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One notation-card audit for the filled line bundle and Berry connection.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One notation-card audit for the filled line bundle and Berry connection.

title: Chern-Simons Gauge Variation Is a Boundary Term
summary: For abelian U(1) Chern-Simons theory, a gauge transformation A -> A + dLambda changes the action by a boundary integral, which is the anomaly-inflow term on manifolds with boundary.

title: Clock-Shift Algebra Has an r-Dimensional Irreducible Representation
summary: If A and B obey AB = omega BA with omega^r = 1 primitive, then repeated action of A cycles the r eigenvalues of B and generates an r-dimensional irreducible representation.

title: Hemisphere Berry Flux of a Massive Dirac Hamiltonian Gives Half a Chern Number
summary: The lower band of H = p_x sigma_x + p_y sigma_y + m sigma_z defines a map from the compactified momentum plane to one hemisphere of S^2, so its Berry flux is one half of the sphere degree and equals sign(m)/2.

title: Large Gauge Transformations Shift I_CS by Two Pi
summary: On S^2 x S^1 with one unit of flux and temporal holonomy parameter s, a large gauge transformation sends s to s + 2pi and shifts I_CS accordingly; source anchor: Lecture Two / Quantization Of The Chern-Simons Coupling (lines 1314-1360; eqs. unitf, nif, nifty, prif).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two proof-fragment audit for the large-gauge-transformation shift of the Chern-Simons action.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two proof-fragment audit for the large-gauge-transformation shift of the Chern-Simons action.

title: Monopole Argument for Fractional Excitations
summary: If the Hall coefficient were noninteger without additional excitations, a spherical Hall sample around a unit monopole would carry noninteger charge, so fractionally charged quasiparticles must appear; source anchor: Lecture Three / More On The Fractional Quantum Hall Effect (lines 1913-1944; eqs. groof, droof).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Three monopole argument audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Three monopole argument audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Projector Formula for the TKNN Chern Number
summary: For the occupied-band projector P(p), the Hall integer is the Brillouin-zone integral of i Tr P [dP, dP] / 2pi, which equals the first Chern class of the occupied bundle.

title: Twist-Angle Torus Tiles the Brillouin Zone Exactly Once
summary: Summing over discrete crystal momenta and integrating the twist angles over 0 <= alpha_i < 2pi is exactly the same as integrating over the full Brillouin zone.

title: Zero Total Winding via Stokes on the Punctured Brillouin Zone
summary: Using the closed-form identity for n and Stokes's theorem on the punctured Brillouin zone gives sum_alpha w(S_alpha) = 0; source anchor: Lecture One / The Nielsen-Ninomiya Theorem (lines 527-556; eqs. zolf, cl).

coverage.notes: Bounded proof-fragment audit for the Nielsen-Ninomiya Stokes argument.

consensus.notes: Bounded proof-fragment audit for the Nielsen-Ninomiya Stokes argument.

title: Berry Curvature Two-Form
summary: The Berry curvature two-form F is the gauge-invariant curvature of the Berry connection and integrates to the first Chern number on closed surfaces.

title: Chirality Sign
summary: The chirality sign is the plus or minus label attached to a Weyl node by the sign of the Jacobian determinant of the local linearized map.

title: Fermi Energy
summary: The Fermi energy is the reference energy that determines which band crossings control low-energy excitations; a crossing away from it does not automatically define a Weyl semimetal phase.

title: Fermi Velocity
summary: The Fermi velocity is the slope of the dispersion at a crossing and acts as the effective light-cone speed in the linearized continuum description.

title: Winding Number
summary: The winding number is the integer degree assigned to an isolated bad point by the normalized map from an enclosing sphere in momentum space to the target sphere.

title: Isolated Bad-Point Regime for Nielsen-Ninomiya
summary: The Nielsen-Ninomiya argument assumes the band Hamiltonian is gapped except at finitely many isolated bad points in the Brillouin zone; source anchor: Lecture One / The Nielsen-Ninomiya Theorem (lines 455-477).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One regime-card audit for the isolated-bad-point scope used in the Nielsen-Ninomiya argument.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One regime-card audit for the isolated-bad-point scope used in the Nielsen-Ninomiya argument.

title: Witten Topological Phases Lecture One Source Map
summary: This source map records which Lecture One sections and equation labels seed the current canonical object set in the repository.

title: The Jacobian b_ij Determines Weyl Chirality
summary: The matrix b_ij = partial b_i / partial p_j at p* binds the local linearization to chirality through sign det(b_ij); source anchor: Lecture One / Three Dimensions (lines 406-450; eqs. expa, perd).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One Jacobian-chirality audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One Jacobian-chirality audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Boundary Anomaly Inflow Requires Chiral Edge Modes
summary: On a manifold with boundary, the variation of the bulk Chern-Simons term cannot be canceled by a local boundary counterterm and therefore requires anomalous gapless boundary modes; source anchor: Lecture Two / Edge States And Anomaly Inflow (lines 1600-1629; eqs. facs, trytry).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two anomaly-inflow audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two anomaly-inflow audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Chern-Simons Level Must Be an Integer
summary: Because the path-integral phase only requires the action mod 2pi, the coefficient of I_CS must be integral in a microscopic theory with properly quantized charge; source anchor: Lecture Two / Quantization Of The Chern-Simons Coupling (lines 1352-1367).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two abelian Chern-Simons level quantization audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two abelian Chern-Simons level quantization audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Complex AZ Defect Classification by Delta Parity
summary: In the two complex Altland-Zirnbauer classes, class A defects are Z-classified when delta is even and trivial otherwise, while class AIII defects are Z-classified when delta is odd and trivial otherwise; source anchor: Fully gapped free fermion systems and topological defects / Topological defects (lines 2019-2023).

coverage.notes: Bounded theorem audit for the complex-class delta-parity classification rule.

consensus.notes: Bounded theorem audit for the complex-class delta-parity classification rule.

title: Conventional Insulators Are Stably Equivalent to the Vacuum
summary: Under the paper's stable classification that identifies states differing only by trivial core bands, all conventional insulators are equivalent to one another and to the vacuum; source anchor: Topological Band Theory / The insulating state (lines 178-187).

coverage.notes: Bounded theorem audit for Hasan-Kane's coarse stable classification statement that conventional insulators are equivalent to one another and to the vacuum after trivial core bands are ignored.

consensus.notes: Bounded theorem audit for Hasan-Kane's coarse stable classification statement that conventional insulators are equivalent to one another and to the vacuum after trivial core bands are ignored.

title: Graphene Lattice Symmetries Protect the Dirac Nodes
summary: Reflection and rotational symmetries of the honeycomb lattice protect the Dirac nodes and keep them at the same energy until suitable symmetry breaking introduces masses; source anchor: Lecture Three / Haldane's Model Of Graphene (lines 2215-2244).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Three graphene symmetry-protection audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Three graphene symmetry-protection audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: A Massive Two Plus One Dimensional Dirac Fermion Induces a Half-Level Chern-Simons Term
summary: Integrating out a massive 2+1-dimensional Dirac fermion yields (sign m / 2) I_CS(A), with the half-integer coefficient explained by the hemisphere Berry-winding argument; source anchor: Lecture Three / More On Edge States Of The Integer Quantum Hall Effect (lines 2064-2091; eqs. utof, uftof, puft, PPP).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Three half-level Chern-Simons audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Three half-level Chern-Simons audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Hall Level Equals the Many-Body Chern Number
summary: Substituting the twist-angle background into the Chern-Simons response yields hat F = k / 2pi and hence hat k' = k; source anchor: Lecture Two / Proof Of The Equivalence (lines 1576-1597; eqs. mizz, roff, coff, plizz).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Two Hall-level equals many-body Chern-number audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Two Hall-level equals many-body Chern-number audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Jackiw-Rebbi Zero Mode for a Sign-Changing Dirac Mass
summary: If a Dirac mass profile changes sign across an interface, the Dirac operator admits a normalizable bound state whose envelope is the exponential of minus the mass integral.

title: Many-Band Zero Total Winding via Traced Curvature
summary: In the many-band case, the traced U(k) Berry curvature satisfies sum_alpha w_alpha = 0 by the Bianchi identity and Stokes's theorem; source anchor: Lecture One / A System With Many Bands (lines 766-779; eqs. zomo, mpf).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One many-band zero-total-winding audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One many-band zero-total-winding audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Real AZ Classification by s minus Delta
summary: For real Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes, the classification of topological defects depends only on the single congruence class s - delta = s - d + D modulo 8; source anchor: Fully gapped free fermion systems and topological defects / Topological defects (lines 1997-2013).

coverage.notes: Bounded theorem audit for the real-class dependence on s-delta in early Section III.

consensus.notes: Bounded theorem audit for the real-class dependence on s-delta in early Section III.

title: Generic Three-Dimensional Two-Band Crossing Has Codimension Three
summary: For a generic 2x2 Hermitian Hamiltonian, equal eigenvalues impose three real conditions, so isolated crossings are natural in three momentum dimensions; source anchor: Lecture One / Three Dimensions (lines 360-394; eqs. generic, specil, ecial).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture One codimension-three audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture One codimension-three audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Level-r U(1) Chern-Simons Theory Has r Torus Ground States
summary: The irreducible representation of the torus Wilson-loop algebra has dimension r, giving r ground states in the large-system limit; source anchor: Lecture Three / More On The Fractional Quantum Hall Effect (lines 2036-2052; eq. woof and following discussion).

coverage.notes: Bounded Lecture Three torus-degeneracy audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

consensus.notes: Bounded Lecture Three torus-degeneracy audit from the fine-grained Witten 1510.07698 reread.

title: Crossing Away From The Fermi Energy Is Not Yet A Clean Weyl Semimetal
summary: A topological crossing can exist in a band structure without controlling the low-energy physics; to identify a clean Weyl semimetal, the crossing must be relevant near the Fermi energy.

title: Local Weyl Description Does Not Fix Global Lattice Consistency
summary: A local Weyl cone only describes the neighborhood of one crossing; it does not by itself guarantee that a globally periodic lattice model exists with the same isolated chirality assignment.
